title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. tortifolium
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. tortifolium, also known as the rough hawk's beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Hieracium vulgatum subsp. tortifolium include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and support the immune system. Hierbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
